name: Cider Bugs
recipeIngredient: Tissue paper or colored plastic wrap (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the oranges in half and remove as much of the pulp as possible.
2. Place the halved oranges in a preheated oven set to 250°F (125°C) for about 2 hours on warm, then allow them to cool completely.
3. In the empty shell halves, mound brown sugar.
4. Use cinnamon sticks to create legs along the sides of the shells (6-8 depending on size), pushing them into the sugar.
5. Place a whole nutmeg in the sugar as the head and add 2 whole cloves for eyes.
6. Wrap each set of bugs securely in tissue paper or colored plastic wrap, with instructions to drop them into two quarts of cider and heat through.
7. Serve and enjoy! My neighbors love these year after year.
